“Roughly speaking, after the Iran war began and oil prices spiked, Russia’s additional daily oil revenues exceeded the amount of total annual investment in Ukraine’s entire defense-tech sector.” -Sergiy Kostman, Ukrainian defense consultant


Financial Times (@FT)

Moscow has so far earned an estimated $1.3bn-$1.9bn windfall from taxes on oil exports, making it the biggest winner from the conflict in the Middle East.

What does this mean for energy markets and the Russian economy? ft.trib.al/Yhy

The Trump administration either didn’t sufficiently forecast the risk of Russian and Chinese material gains from the Iran war, or didn’t prioritize them.


Javier Blas (@JavierBlas)

US has further eased Russian sanctions, allowing ALL countries (and not just India) to buy Russian oil already loaded by March 12. Vladimir Putin continues to cash in on President Trump's attack on Iran. The Kremlin can sell stranded oil barrels at much higher prices.







— https://nitter.net/JavierBlas/status/20

CENTCOM confirms four of six crewmembers aboard lost KC-135 were killed - search and rescue efforts continue for other two.


U.S. Central Command (@CENTCOM)

Four Confirmed Deceased in Loss of U.S. KC-135 Over Iraq

TAMPA, Fla. – At approximately 2 pm ET on March 12, a U.S. KC-135 refueling aircraft went down in western Iraq. Four of six crew members on board the aircraft have been confirmed deceased as rescue efforts continue.

The circumstances of the incident are under investigation. However

Breaking: @CENTCOM announces loss of KC-135 aircraft over Iraq, rescue efforts still ongoing.

Full statement: 

Loss of U.S. KC-135 Over Iraq

March 12, 2026
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

TAMPA, Fla. – U.S. Central Command is aware of the loss of a U.S. KC-135 refueling aircraft. The incident occurred in friendly airspace during Operation Epic Fury, and rescue efforts are ongoing. Two aircraft were involved in the incident. One of the aircraft went down in western Iraq, and the second landed safely.

This is quite a power flex by Iran and China:


ian bremmer (@ianbremmer)

the straits of hormuz aren’t completely closed. 

iranian tankers are still passing through—bringing oil to china and oil revenue to the islamic republic. 

the united states could stop them. but that would antagonize china and further escalate the war. so…here we are.



— https://nitter.net/ianbremmer/status/2032106029060636989#m

New: Oil prices surged and brent crude settled above $100 per barrel for the first time since 2022 as the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz continues to roil markets. 

Brent crude, the international oil benchmark, rose 9.22%, to settle at $100.46 per barrel. It’s the first time Brent has settled above $100 per barrel since August 2022. 
-@CNN

Breaking: Suspect dead after ramming vehicle into Detroit-area synagogue, sheriff says
Emergency responders found what appeared to be a large amount of explosives in the back of the vehicle, according to law enforcement officials.
- @CNN

Helsinki 2.0. He knows - or should know - that the Iran-Russia relationship extends to the very threats this administration claims to be addressing: Tehran’s missile and nuclear programs.


Aaron Rupar (@atrupar)

QUINTANILLA: Do we think the Russians have shared intel about the location of US military assets, and if so, why would we be giving waivers on Russian oil sanctions?

WITKOFF: I'm not an intel officer, so I can't tell you. I can tell you that on the call with the president, the Russian

Four years ago today, I met @chefjoseandres in Ukraine as he was already feeding the victims of Russia’s war. It happened to be my birthday. I couldn’t think of a better way to spend another birthday than seeing him honored for his and @WCKitchen’s work by @YaleSOM. Thank you for your service and inspiration!
Trump has kind words for both Putin and Xi, saying Putin wants to be “constructive” in Ukraine peace talks and that it’s “an honor” for him to help China by easing oil traffic through the Gulf.

Trump says that without US military action last year Iran “would have had a nuclear weapon and would have used it before this press conference.” The administration has not offered any intelligence or assessment to support this claim.
Trump just used a new word for the war in Iran: “an excursion”.

And now: “We could call it a tremendous success or we could further and we’re going to go further.”


Jim Sciutto (@jimsciutto)

Trump says the war with Iran will be over “very soon”.



— https://nitter.net/jimsciutto/status/2031130280518390232#m
